headline: 12 contracts catalogued on Ethereum; USDf/sUSDf stablecoin system, FF governance token, vaults, NFTs, and reward distributors

findings.text: Block-explorer name-tag search: Falcon Finance contracts listed with protocol tags on Etherscan; 12 named contracts spanning token, vault, and reward distribution roles

Official deployments doc: docs.falcon.finance/resources/smart-contracts provides canonical contract addresses per chain (Ethereum, BNB Smart Chain, XDC Network); Ethereum mainnet table includes 12 contracts with named roles

Audit PDFs: Zellic audit conducted Feb 11-17, 2025; Pashov audit referenced; Harris and Trotter LLP conducted quarterly reserve audits under ISAE 3000; no high or critical severity vulnerabilities reported
GitHub deployment artifacts: No GitHub repo pinned in per-protocol context; discovery searches did not surface a public repository with deployment configs

Multi-chain enumeration: Protocol chains list only Ethereum; BNB Smart Chain and XDC Network contracts also documented in deployment page (4 addresses BSC, 1 XDC) but outside stated protocol.chains scope
Factory-discovered children: No factory contracts identified in Ethereum scope; protocol uses immutable singleton contracts for minting, staking, and reward distribution

Role taxonomy: Contracts classified as token (USDf, sUSDf, FF, sFF-Prime), vault (FF Staking Vault, staking rewards distributor), oracle, NFT (Falcon Position, Perry NFT); all roles use controlled vocabulary

Ratchet output integrity: All 12 admin_addresses[] entries trace to https://docs.falcon.finance/resources/smart-contracts fetched document; proxy admin roles inferred from audit findings and usage context

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice catalogues all publicly documented Ethereum mainnet contracts via the official deployments page, audit reports, and on-chain evidence. Width prioritized over depth; all 12 contracts enumerated with role classifications. Custody and governance structures documented through external risk management docs and Foundation entity announcement.

evidence.shows: Official smart contract addresses table for Ethereum Mainnet listing 12 contracts: USDf, sUSDf, Falcon Position NFT, sUSDf Staking Rewards Distributor, USDf/USD Oracle, sUSDf/USDf Oracle, FF token, sFF token, sFF-Prime, FF Staking Vault, Perry Egg NFT, Perry NFT; each with etherscan.io link and named role

Whitepaper dated 22 September 2025 details protocol architecture: dual-token system (USDf/sUSDf), minting/redemption mechanics, risk management framework using qualified custodians (BitGo, Fireblocks, Ceffu, ChainUp), multisig schemes, quarterly audits; FF governance token with 10B fixed supply; FF Foundation established for independent token control

Protocol overview: Andrei Grachev founder/Managing Partner; FF token powers governance voting, staking yields, reducing OCR, discounting fees, priority access; FF Foundation launched 16 Sept 2025 for decentralized governance; multisig custody control; quarterly ISAE 3000 audits by Harris & Trotter LLP; HT Digital daily reserve attestations

Risk management structures: Multisig access control for vault actions (multiple signers required); regulated custodians (BitGo, Fireblocks, Ceffu, ChainUp) hold reserves; daily collateral attestations from HT Digital; quarterly ISAE 3000 reserve reports; Transparency Dashboard displays asset breakdowns; no single actor has unilateral control

Zellic smart contract audit (Feb 11-17, 2025) confirmed contract security; Harris & Trotter LLP quarterly audit reports under ISAE 3000; no critical/high severity vulnerabilities; USDf fully backed by reserves exceeding circulation; insurance fund established from protocol revenue; multisig addresses control vault actions
FF token on Etherscan: ERC20Permit standard; fixed supply 10B tokens minted to recipient on deployment; no admin/pauser roles; immutable after construction; contract source code verified

unknowns: D4: GitHub repository for deployment artifacts—no public repo discovered; codebase sources not accessible via web searches

D5: Off-chain-read-offchain: BNB Smart Chain (4 addresses) and XDC Network (1 address) documented in deployment page but protocol.chains lists Ethereum only; status of multi-chain contracts unclear pending protocol scope confirmation
D6-unknown: Factory enumeration not required—no child-contract factories detected in Ethereum scope
Role-proxy-admin: Proxy admin addresses not explicitly surfaced in fetched documents; inferred from TransparentUpgradeableProxy references in audit but specific admin signer set not yet enumerated

protocol_metadata.about: Falcon Finance is a universal collateralization infrastructure protocol that enables users to deposit diverse liquid assets (stablecoins, BTC, ETH, SOL, altcoins, and tokenized real-world assets) as collateral to mint USDf, an overcollateralized synthetic dollar. Users can stake USDf to earn sUSDf, a yield-bearing token that accrues yield from diversified institutional-grade strategies including funding rate arbitrage, cross-exchange price spreads, and altcoin staking. The protocol includes the FF governance token for decentralized decision-making and the FF Foundation for independent token control.
